_meta.name: Poindexter core seed
_meta.description: Free-tier starter settings. The brain bootstraps this into app_settings on first boot when the table is empty. Designed to be the minimum viable config to make the pipeline runnable â€” docker compose up, visit /api/health, submit a task. For the optimized production seed (site-specific models, tuned QA workflows, image styles, writing styles), see the premium Quick Start Guide product on Lemon Squeezy.

settings.description: Brand/site name used across all services
Production domain (no protocol)
Full production URL with protocol
Longer site description used in meta tags
Short tagline used in metadata
Legal company name
Founder name used in writer prompts
Team size for content validation
Comma-separated real products (for hallucination checks)
Support contact email
Privacy/GDPR contact email
Site owner email
Site owner display name
Backend API base URL (in-network for Docker)
Customer-facing site URL
Ollama API endpoint (uses host.docker.internal so containers can reach host Ollama)

Docker Compose project name used by the brain's compose drift auto-recover when calling docker compose up from inside the container. Set to the basename of the host directory that launched the stack (e.g. "glad-labs-website" if you cloned into ~/glad-labs-website/). Leave empty to let docker compose infer the name from the compose file path, which works when the brain container and host share the same working directory.

Host directory that owns docker-compose.local.yml, passed to `docker compose --project-directory` during the brain's compose drift auto-recover. The brain bind-mounts only the compose file (at /app/docker-compose.local.yml), so relative bind-mount sources like ./infrastructure/prometheus/prometheus.yml would otherwise resolve against /app and either fail the recreate or wipe the service's real config with an empty auto-created dir. Set to the absolute HOST path of the repo dir (e.g. "/home/you/glad-labs-website"). Leave empty to auto-derive it from the running container's com.docker.compose.project.working_dir label (zero-config); only set it explicitly if that label is missing or wrong.
Primary content generation model. gemma3:27b is the free-tier default (widely available, no specialized hardware required)
QA/content review model
Fallback model when primary is unavailable
SEO title/description generation (smaller, faster)
Social media post generation
Content refinement stage. Premium seed swaps this for a higher-capability model.
Research stage. Premium seed swaps this for a higher-capability model.
Fact-checking mode: 'programmatic' or an LLM provider
Free-tier default writer. Premium seed upgrades to a specialized creative-writing model.
Quality scoring / structured JSON output
Fact-checking. Kept separate from writer on purpose.
SEO / metadata. Small model is fine.
Generates SDXL prompts from post content
Fast summaries, short-form copy
Default cost tier (free/budget/standard/premium/flagship)
Cloud API mode: disabled, emergency_only, fallback, always. Free tier runs Ollama-only.
Hard cap on cloud API calls per day in emergency mode
Minimum overall QA score (0-100) to pass
Multi-model QA final approval threshold
Minimum score on any single dimension
Weight for programmatic validator
Weight for LLM critic
Promote validator warnings to critical when any single rule exceeds this count (GH-91)
Points subtracted from final QA score per validator warning (GH-91)
Auto-publish score threshold (0 = disabled, human approval required)
All publishes require human approval
Posts go to draft + preview token instead of publishing
Minimum quality to even queue for approval
Minimum QA score to surface for review
Hard cap on publishes per day
Soft cap on generation per day
Maximum new posts per week (0 = unlimited)
Queue size before generation throttles
Max retry attempts for failed tasks
Max tokens per LLM call
Max tokens summed across a whole task
Minimum hours between publishes
Days before unapproved posts auto-expire
Task considered stale after this
Seconds between stale-task sweeps
Hard cap on daily AI spend in USD (read by services/cost_guard.py)
Hard cap on monthly AI spend in USD (read by services/cost_guard.py)
Alert when spend exceeds this % of limit
Enable agent memory (pgvector)
Enable Model Context Protocol server
Capture training data from pipeline runs
Enable Redis for caching and pub/sub
Minimum word count for blog posts
Target word count for blog posts
Max refinement iterations
Temperature for content generation
Temperature for QA review
Primary image source: 'pexels' or 'ai_generation'

AI image generation model

settings.value: text, words, letters, numbers, watermark, signature, logo, face, person, human, hands, fingers, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed
settings.description: Negative prompt for all SDXL generations

settings.value: professional digital art, abstract technology concept, blue and cyan color scheme, clean modern aesthetic, no people, no text, no faces, no letters
settings.description: Default SDXL style for uncategorized posts
Seconds without an embeddings_total change before EmbeddingsStale fires (default 6h)
Daily LLM spend (USD) that triggers a warning alert
Daily LLM spend (USD) that triggers a critical alert â€” pipeline should have already stopped
Monthly LLM spend (USD) that triggers a warning alert
Per-rail skip ratio that fires QaRailFullySkipped (1 = skipped 100% of the last N QA passes; lower e.g. 0.9 to page earlier) — poindexter#553
Postgres SELECT 1 p99 latency (seconds) that fires PostgresSlowQuery (default 100ms)
Number of published posts without embeddings that fires PostsNotEmbedded
GPU temperature (°C) that fires GpuTemperatureHigh — safe ceiling below the ~90°C throttle point
GPU VRAM utilization % that fires GpuVramHigh — 95% signals OOM-imminent before Ollama/SDXL starts failing
Host disk free space (GB) that fires PoindexterDiskSpaceLow warning alert
Host disk free space (GB) that fires PoindexterDiskSpaceCritical alert — Postgres writes and Docker pulls start failing below this
Minimum total disk size (GB) for disk-space alerts — filters out USB drives and EFI partitions that are always near-full
Postgres connection utilization ratio (0–1) that fires PoindexterPostgresConnectionsHigh warning — 0.80 gives a 5-min buffer before asyncpg starts rejecting
Postgres connection utilization ratio (0–1) that fires PoindexterPostgresConnectionsCritical — shed load immediately at this level
Error count in the rolling window that fires brain-high-error-rate (alert #1)
SQL INTERVAL hours for the brain-high-error-rate rolling window (alert #1)
SQL INTERVAL hours before an in_progress pipeline_tasks row is considered stale (alert #2 brain-stale-tasks)
Hours since newest embedding before brain-embedding-sync-lag fires (alert #3)
Postgres DB size (GB) that fires brain-db-size-warning (alert #4)
Rolling LLM spend (USD) that fires brain-daily-cost-spike (alert #5)
SQL INTERVAL hours for the brain-daily-cost-spike rolling window (alert #5)
Minimum average quality score before brain-content-quality-drop fires (alert #6)
SQL INTERVAL days for the brain-content-quality-drop rolling window (alert #6)
Traffic ratio below which brain-traffic-anomaly fires (0.5 = 50% drop vs prior period, alert #7)
Minimum GSC clicks required for brain-page-views-capture-dead check (alert #7b) — low-traffic pages exempt
SQL INTERVAL hours without local inference before brain-ollama-down fires (alert #10)
Minutes since last brain_decision before brain-heartbeat-stale fires (alert #11)
Minutes since last gpu_metrics row before brain-gpu-metrics-stale fires (alert #13)
GPU temperature (°C) that fires brain-gpu-temp-high SQL alert (alert #14) — complements the Prometheus GpuTemperatureHigh rule
Config for RenderGrafanaAlertsJob — renders grafana.threshold.* settings into alert-rules.yml and triggers Grafana provisioning reload
How many recent QA passes the poindexter_qa_rail_skip_ratio gauge measures a rail's skip rate over â€” poindexter#553
Config for RenderPrometheusRulesJob â€” renders app_settings rules into the Prometheus rules dir and hits /-/reload on change
Config for RenderAlertmanagerConfigJob (#524) â€” renders alertmanager.yml.tmpl with telegram_chat_id and hits /-/reload on change (delivery-plane dead-man's switch)

Ordered list of Stage names the content pipeline runs. Operators can disable (drop from list), reorder, or insert third-party stages without a code deploy. Falls back to DEFAULT_STAGE_ORDER if missing.
Enable OTel tracing. When true, services/telemetry.py wires the worker's FastAPI + LLMProvider-dispatch spans out to otel_exporter_otlp_endpoint.
Where services/telemetry.py exports traces. Default points at the Tempo container in docker-compose.local.yml. Override with 'https://api.honeycomb.io/v1/traces' etc. for managed backends.
Opt in to continuous profiling via the pyroscope-io Python agent. Agent config (server URL, app name, sample rate) is read at worker startup. Keep false on resource-constrained hosts.
URL the Python agent ingests profiles to. Default points at the pyroscope container in docker-compose.local.yml.

Sentry-compatible DSN. Leave empty to disable error tracking. Point at a GlitchTip DSN (format: http://<public_key>@glitchtip-web:8000/<project_id>) to route worker exceptions to the self-hosted GlitchTip container.
Master switch for services/sentry_integration.py. Takes sentry_dsn as input; sentry_dsn='' short-circuits this anyway.
